title: The AI ​​Colleague we want to create
description: Moonlight is an AI Colleague that researches together
author: 김선영(HR Manager)
searchText: Moonlight는 새롭게 발표되는 논문을 더 빠르게 이해할 수 있도록 논문 리뷰 페이지를 제공합니다. 논문의 핵심 내용과 기여를 정리하고 관련 연구를 함께 살펴볼 수 있도록 도와 연구자가 논문을 읽기 전에 전체적인 맥락을 빠르게 파악할 수 있도록 만드는 것이 목표입니다. 얼마 전 한 연구자로부터 메일 한 통을 받았습니다. 자신의 논문 리뷰 페이지를 읽어봤는데 해외 유명 대학의 리뷰 서비스 (Stanford Agentic Review) 보다 논문의 맥락을 훨씬 잘 이해하고 있어 인상적이었다는 내용이었습니다. 제품을 만드는 팀으로서는 정말 기쁜 피드백이었습니다. 하지만 우리에게는 그 다음에 이어진 조언이 더 인상적이었습니다. _You write: stop struggling with complex Lie algebras and let AI work for you. I am sure it was meant as a light-hearted comment. Everything is a result of personal struggles and we do mathematics to satisfy our desire to know. We cannot delegate understanding to a machine. (복잡한 Lie 대수와 씨름하지 말고 AI에게 맡기라는 표현은 가볍게 쓴 문구라는 점은 이해합니다. 하지만 모든 것은 스스로 고민하고 탐구한 결과이며 우리는 알고자 하는 마음 때문에 연구합니다. 이해를 기계에 위임할 수는 없습니다.)_ 메일을 다시 읽어보니 우리 리뷰 페이지에는 이런 추천 문구가 사용되고 있었습니다. Stop struggling with complex Lie algebras and let AI work for you. (복잡한 Lie 대수와 씨름하지 말고 AI에게 맡기세요.) 원래 의도는 AI가 복잡한 연구 내용을 정리하고 이해를 돕는다는 의미였습니다. 그런데 메일을 읽고 보니, 우리가 정말 전하고 싶었던 메시지와는 조금 달랐습니다. 우리는 Moonlight를 단순히 질문에 답해주는 AI Assistant로 만들고 싶지 않습니다. 그렇다고 연구를 대신 수행하는 AI Agent를 만들고 싶은 것도 아닙니다. 연구자가 논문과 직접 부딪히며 얻는 이해까지 AI가 대신해서는 안 된다고 생각하기 때문입니다. 우리가 만들고 싶은 것은 함께 연구하는 AI Colleague 입니다. Moonlight가 집중하는 것은 AI가 얼마나 많은 일을 대신할 수 있는지가 아닙니다. 연구자가 논문을 읽는 과정에서 더 좋은 질문을 떠올리고, 주장과 근거를 더 분명히 붙잡고, 다음에 무엇을 읽고 판단할지 더 빠르고 자신 있게 결정하도록 돕는 것입니다. 더 나아가 우리는 Moonlight가 논문을 읽는 순간에만 머무르지 않고, 연구자가 아이디어를 정리하고, 관련 연구를 탐색하고, 다음 연구 방향을 결정하는 전체 과정에서도 함께할 수 있기를 꿈꾸고 있습니다. 그래서 문구 하나만 수정하고 끝내지 않았습니다. 먼저 이 문구가 왜 생성됐는지 추천 문구 생성 프롬프트를 다시 살펴봤습니다. 그리고 Moonlight가 연구자에게 전달하고 싶은 메시지에 더 가깝도록 해당 리뷰 페이지의 추천 문구를 수정했습니다. 수정한 내용을 반영한 뒤에는 연구자에게도 다시 메일을 보냈습니다. 피드백 덕분에 추천 문구를 수정했고 Moonlight는 연구를 대신하는 AI가 아니라 연구를 돕는 동료를 만들고 싶다는 우리의 생각도 함께 전했습니다. 이번 일을 계기로 우리가 전달하고 싶은 메시지가 추천 문구에도 더 잘 담겨야 한다는 점을 다시 한번 생각하게 되었습니다. Moonlight는 앞으로도 연구자를 대신하는 AI가 아니라 연구를 더 깊고 즐겁게 만들어주는 동료 가 되고자 합니다. 제품을 만드는 입장에서는 기능에 대한 의견만큼이나 우리가 어떤 태도와 언어로 연구자에게 다가가야 하는지 알려주는 피드백도 소중합니다. 이번 메일도 Moonlight를 한 번 더 돌아보게 만든 계기였습니다.

title: Corca's new adaptation period
description: A record of Corca's new employee's first job while adapting to the company, and what he or she learned in the process.
author: 문정민(AX Consultant)
searchText: Ceal-terview·Interview Adapting as a new employee wasn't something I learned quickly on my own. Ceal · July 9, 2026 · AX Sharing Society Interview 🎤Ceal-terview Corca in-house AI agent Ceal This article is a summary of the experiments and trials and errors that emerged from this AX sharing session. AX Sharing Society is a place where Corca members share AI/AX utilization experiments, business application cases, trial and error, and learning. Interviewer: Ceal Interviewee: Moon Jeong-min Interview Topic: The process by which a new employee adapts to blog improvement tasks, gains context through conversation, and creates next milestones in handwriting with an agent. Hello. In-house AI agent that questions and organizes members’ experiences at Corca Ceal It is. In this Ceal-terview, I met Jeongmin Moon and asked him again about the “new employee’s adaptation process to work” that we discussed at the AX sharing session. At first, it was more of a story about how to implement blog improvement tasks. However, as the interview continued, the questions changed little by little. In order for this result to actually be used within the organization, what flow needs to change? I liked this spot. Jeongmin’s story is that adapting as a new employee is not something that can be done quickly and well on one’s own. The process of talking with people, expanding your thoughts with tools, and finally organizing them in your own language. Because it shows. 👤 Interviewee introduction Jeongmin Moon is a member of the Corca AX team. In this interview, he shared his experience working on improving a blog and re-understanding what initially seemed to be a focus on implementation and automation as a flow that is actually used within the organization. In particular, Jeongmin talked about how he learned the context of his work as a new employee by using a combination of pair work, conversation, using AI agents, and organizing handwriting. 💬 Q1. What problem did you initially see with this? The first thing I saw from Jeongmin was the specific task of improving the blog. Whether to implement a blog, automate publishing through a bot, and how to post posts. The first thought naturally was, “What should I make?” headed towards. “At first we thought about automation, such as implementing blogs or publishing through bots, but that was simply one way to do it.” What I caught from this answer is one way That was the expression. Implementation was necessary, but implementation alone was not sufficient. Improving a blog was not about creating a screen or automation for posting posts, but creating a flow in which posts were created, shared, and refined within the organization. 처음 질문: 무엇을 만들면 될까? 바뀐 질문: 이 흐름이 조직 안에서 쓰이려면 무엇이 필요할까? 💬 Q2. Since when did you feel that you needed to look at organizational change rather than just simple implementation? Jeongmin said that the size of the problem changed through pair work and conversation. “Through working in pairs and talking with other team members, I learned that the goal is to bring about organizational change within the topic of improving the blog.” The important words in this answer are organizational change It was. At first I thought about implementing a blog. We then specified the necessary steps. And at the end, we got to see the entire process of introducing the organization. 📝 Ceal's Note The difficult thing for newbies is not just not knowing the technology. What is more often difficult is that we do not yet know how the technology will be used within the company. 💬 Q3. What were the limitations of thinking alone for long periods of time? Newcomers need time to think alone for a long time. But corporate context did not come quickly just by sitting alone. “From the perspective of a new employee, the context of the existing company, the way it communicates, and the way it conducts work were not yet well understood.” When it comes to implementation issues, the more you dig into it on your own, the closer you get to the answer. But the adoption problem is different. Who uses this flow, where it clashes with existing methods, and at what moments people might stop. These questions become clear when you encounter other people's experiences and expectations. 💬 Ceal’s Observations Good questions help me find answers quickly, but more importantly, I think they change the size of the problem I'm looking at. 💬 Q4. So how have you actually adapted? When Jeongmin's thoughts became more concrete or concerns arose, he started talking about them with others rather than keeping them to himself for longer. “Rather than thinking about it alone for a long time, I found that it is much more efficient and faster to discuss it with others once my thoughts have become more concrete and I have concerns.” An AI agent was also used here. The agent helped me quickly organize unfamiliar concepts, compare possible approaches, and broaden my thinking. However, Jeongmin did not stop at the answer given by the agent. “I felt that it was good to learn while actively using the agent. However, it was more effective to organize my thoughts at the end by thinking directly and writing them down by hand.” 사람과 대화한다 → 회사의 맥락을 얻는다 에이전트에게 묻는다 → 생각의 선택지를 넓힌다 손으로 다시 쓴다 → 다음 마일스톤을 내 언어로 정한다 👉 Jeongmin’s adaptation routine When I have concerns, I don't decide on an answer right away. Gain context about the company through pair work and conversation. As an agent, you quickly expand unfamiliar concepts and options. Determine the next milestone by rewriting it by hand. 💬 Q5. If you could boil this experience down to one sentence principle, what would it be? Jeongmin responded that this experience was “a process of adapting to the job from the perspective of a new employee.” I liked this expression. This is because we do not view adaptation as just “doing things quickly and well alone.” This is because, rather than working with everything you know from the beginning, it is a process that starts with implementation, specifies the steps, and then looks at the entire process of introducing the organization. Adaptation may not mean hiding what you don't know and enduring it alone. It could be having a conversation at the moment of need, learning quickly with a tool, and then organizing it with your own hands to create the next action. 🧭 Read and try it right away 1. First ask about the flow behind the implementation task If your current task seems like a simple implementation, you might want to ask this question first. What flow should this result go into for it to actually be used within the organization? Creating a feature and making it usable are two different issues. The newer you are, the more helpful it is to quickly identify this difference through conversation. 2. Share the stuck points rather than the completed idea. It takes a long time to fully organize your thoughts and then share them. Rather, it may be quicker to have a conversation when things have been sorted out to some extent, or when you see a sticking point. I'm currently looking at this as an implementation issue. Is there anything I'm missing from an adoption or operational perspective? 3. Rewrite the answer you received from the agent by hand If you leave the answer given by the agent as is, it becomes information. If I rewrite it by hand, it will be my judgment. What I learned today, what I don't know yet, and what I'll check next time. Just three things are enough. ✅ Small checkpoints Can you describe the work I am currently responsible for as “flow within the organization” rather than “implementation of functions”? Instead of thinking about it alone for a long time, what kind of questions can you talk to to make the size of the problem clearer? Have I reorganized the answers I received from my agent into the next milestone in my language? 🌱 In conclusion There is one sentence that Ceal took away from this interview. Adapting as a new employee is not a matter of learning quickly on your own; it is a process of re-learning the size of the problem. Jeongmin gained context from people, expanded his thoughts as an agent, and reorganized them by hand to make it his own work. Even in an era where AI takes over work, what is ultimately important may be the way we connect. Who to ask, what tools to use to expand your thoughts, and how to grasp them again in your own words in the end. Jeongmin’s story clearly showed that trend.

title: Don't say something you haven't confirmed as fact
description: Based on the AX sharing session and experience using Ceal, we summarize the working principles and CLI operating rules that prevent AI from saying unconfirmed information as fact.
author: 김예린
searchText: Don't say something you haven't confirmed as fact While Yerin Kim was planning a 2nd anniversary event with AI, what she needed before the answer was “It is possible.” He said it was an attitude that revealed the actual scope of verification. This article starts from that scene, Organizes the framework of reliability guidelines to be used when performing CLI operations in the moonlight repo. Based on presenter interview Topic: AI task reliability Target: moonlight repo CLI operation instructions One chapter summary. The emotion of the presentation case is shown first. It does not end with “AI was implemented quickly”, After review, DB, and risky work confirmation No guessing, check DB first, read reviews first It is a trend that changes with the rule. What was the actual scene that made you think, “I need to leave this as a guideline”? The problem was “pretending to check” rather than a wrong answer. One of the important conditions when planning the 2nd anniversary event was to minimize the load on the operating user database. So, Yerin does not directly query or repeatedly read the DB for AI. We asked them to consider together possible methods within the current structure. However, AI does not check the actual user DB structure or stored data. He answered, “It can be implemented by using the user DB.” whether the required data is actually stored; Without checking whether the required column exists or whether there is a cache that can be used without querying the operational DB Possibilities were treated as if they were facts. The reason trust was shaken in this scene was not just because the AI was wrong. Later, when the AI says, “I checked. Now I’m correct, and I’ll also correct what I said incorrectly in one place earlier.” As a listener, these questions naturally arise. “Then what did you base your statement on earlier?” If the previous answer was a guess, you should have said it was a guess. Even if it is a highly probable decision, I had to separate the facts that were actually confirmed from the parts that had not yet been confirmed. What should we have at least checked before saying AI is “possible”? Two things you need to know before you know it's possible The minimum verification standard chosen by Yerin was simple. First, whether the required column actually exists. Second, is there cache or existing aggregate data that can be used without directly querying the operational DB? required column Check whether the information necessary for event implementation is in the actual schema. Data source without lookup Find cache or aggregate data that can be used without repeatedly querying the operational DB. check border If you haven't seen it yet, say "I haven't confirmed it yet." Good AI collaboration doesn’t end with quickly talking about “plausible implementation methods.” Especially when there is something that requires careful attention, such as operational data, we first ask about the boundaries of information that can be safely verified. Judgment must be made within those boundaries. What would the AI have said back then to make it more trustworthy? Separate claims, facts, and unknowns The expression proposed by Yerin becomes the core principle of this article. Do not speak of unconfirmed information as if it were fact, but distinguish between “claims” and “facts.” 유저 DB에 필요한 데이터가 있을 가능성이 높습니다. — 주장 하지만 필요한 컬럼 존재 여부와, 운영 DB 조회 없이 사용할 수 있는 캐시/집계 데이터가 있는지는 아직 확인하지 못했습니다. — 미확인 상태 다음으로 확인할 것은 필요한 컬럼과 조회 없이 사용할 수 있는 데이터 소스입니다. — 다음 행동 This format is not a device for making the answer longer. Rather, reduce unnecessary explanations, It is a minimal sign that helps users quickly decide what they can trust now. If this guide is well-made, how should AI answers change? The criterion for success is not “more form” but “clearer evidence” Yerin said that it would be nice to be able to see which skill sentence is being referenced in the AI answer. For example, “This /rule-check As in, “I answered by referring to #12 of .” It is a method of briefly revealing the location of the guideline on which it is based. However, this sign is not a decoration that must be attached every time. The moment the boundaries of trust can be shaken, This is especially important in the moments when you say, for example, “I checked,” “I reflected,” or “I’m done.” The user must be able to know whether the expression was accompanied by actual reading, modification, execution, or verification. Briefly reveal the location of the guidance on which it is based when necessary. Distinguish between claims, confirmed facts, and unconfirmed status. Do not say “complete,” “reflect,” or “confirm” without actual confirmation. Rather than asking questions again about what has already been answered, proceed to the next action or product. Quick check: What answer preserves trust in this scene? next question What side effects must be avoided when applying the guidelines? It must be short. If only the format remains, it is a failure. Just because guidelines are in place, not all answers should become lengthy. You should also avoid formally adding just a rule number each time. The important thing is not to “pretend to follow the rules,” but to make the actual work status and rationale more transparent. At some moments, one sentence, “I won’t make any conclusions because it’s not confirmed,” is enough. Conversely, at the moment when you have to say it's complete, what did you read, what did you change, You must briefly disclose what verification was done. Guidelines are not a device to increase the amount of speech; It is a device that distinguishes between what can be neat and what cannot be neat. What is this skill used for? Not for Ceal, but a skill used when working with CLI in the moonlight repo There was one point during the interview where the flow went astray. I tried to explain this guideline as a skill for Ceal runtime response, but Yerin corrected it. This skill is not for Ceal itself, These are the work instructions that Yerin will refer to when turning on the CLI and working in the moonlight repo. Therefore, the focus is not “How will Ceal respond nicely?” The issue lies in how CLI workers transparently express judgment, rationale, progress status, and completion during work. Skill draft direction for moonlight repo Purpose: It distinguishes between actual performance and unconfirmed status during CLI operations, allowing users to trust the status of the operation. Scope of application: The whole process of turning on and working with the CLI in the moonlight repo. Default output: Conclusion → Guidelines or confirmations based on → Next action. Trust boundary: If necessary, include confirmed and unconfirmed information in the explanation of the evidence. Prohibited: Do not say “I have confirmed,” “I have reflected,” or “I have completed” without actual confirmation. Progress principles: Once the necessary judgments have been made, move on to the next step of creating the output or actually reflecting it, rather than repeating the summary. quiet checkpoint The next time an AI says, “I got it,” what should you ask first? View commentary The first question is “What did you check and how?” Like files checked, commands executed, documents viewed, and results verified. There must be a rationale that leads to real action. If there is no basis, the answer should be treated as a claim or assumption rather than a fact. What should colleagues who read this do differently? Before completion, state the basis for completion The conclusion of this article is simple. When working with AI and CLI, you need to be more careful when using “I can” or “I’ve done it.” If there is a possibility, say it is a possibility, and if it is true, say what kind of confirmed fact it is. If it has not been confirmed yet, it is said to be unconfirmed. That way, users can check whether the AI is really working efficiently, You can determine whether you are proceeding in the manner instructed. Trust does not come from fancy expressions. Trust arises when workers clearly distinguish between what they know and what they do not know. Publication notes: This draft is for internal sharing. Details related to operational DB, user data, and internal repo structure are provided. When disclosing to the outside world, it is safer to generalize or anonymize the information. Follow-up action: After checking the actual deployment location in the moonlight repo, condense and organize this content into a skill document for the repo.

title: A new colleague on the operations team, Ceal
description: We introduce an example of how the operations team used Ceal to automate repetitive operational tasks such as corporate cards, surveys, and birthday/employment anniversary notices.
author: 김선영(HR Manager)
searchText: A new colleague on the operations team, Ceal 2026-05-13 Sunyoung Kim (HR Manager) Corca understands the company's operating context. We perform everything from repetitive request processing to operational tasks such as information organizing, document creation, surveys, and announcements. AI co-worker Ceal There is this. Corca is applying Ceal to actual work flows to streamline various operational tasks. In this article, we will introduce how the operations team uses Ceal, focusing on actual use cases. 1. Management of corporate card usage details We manage corporate card usage details through Ceal. If you upload a receipt and the number of users together on a Slack channel, Ceal reads the receipt image and automatically extracts information such as the date of expenditure, name of expenditure, location of use, and amount. Afterwards, the details are automatically recorded in the backlog sheet, and the processing results are also notified through thread comments. If it is treated as an organizational expense, the remaining budget based on the current quarter, the next recharge date and recharge amount, and the reset schedule are also calculated and provided. You can immediately understand the budget status of each team without having to check it separately. We also use Ceal to manage breakfast costs. At Corca, members can use breakfast by paying individually with a corporate card. When you upload a purchase receipt to a message thread that is automatically sent at 8 a.m., Ceal reads the receipt information and automatically records and manages it. Previously, people had to manually enter and organize receipts, but now most repetitive management tasks can be automated. Automatically records receipt information 2. Anonymous survey operation and summary of results The method of conducting surveys has also been greatly simplified through Ceal. Previously, it was necessary to manually create a Google form, provide a link, and then have a person re-read and summarize the submitted responses. Now, Ceal creates and sends surveys right from Slack. Members can complete the survey submission by simply leaving a reply in the form of a comment to the DM sent by Ceal. Participation is now easier than ever, with no need to open separate links or use new tools. Ceal simply collects responses and automatically organizes them into summaries and key comments based on what's submitted. By summarizing satisfaction, repeatedly mentioned opinions, and improvement ideas in one place, we help operators quickly understand the results. Survey via Slack sent by Ceal 3. Automation of notification of birthdays and joining anniversaries Ceal also automatically announces members' birthdays and joining anniversaries. Depending on whether the target person is one person or multiple people, or whether their birthday and company anniversary are both on the same day, the message will be formatted differently and guided through the Slack channel. It not only displays the date, but also generates phrases and mentions that fit the situation. The operations team was able to reduce the repetitive work of checking schedules and writing announcement messages every time, and members were able to celebrate important anniversaries together without missing them. Ceal is not just a chatbot that answers questions. AI co-workers who handle repetitive tasks together within the actual operational flow It is being utilized. With Ceal performing tasks that were previously handled repeatedly by humans, such as organizing receipts, administering surveys, and creating notices, the operations team was able to focus on more important tasks. In the future, Corca plans to apply Ceal to various operational tasks and create a more efficient work environment.

title: VoC-responsive agent builder
description: We introduce how the Moonlight team structured the VoC response process into an agent, reducing the cost of searching for evidence and drafting responses.
author: 하동훈(Software Engineer)
searchText: VoC-responsive agent builder 2026-04-22 Donghoon Ha (Software Engineer) Entering Recently, the Moonlight team has been handling the VoC response process with agents. In this article, I will try to summarize what problems I saw in the process and how I solved them. The reason I started this project was because a small team was handling numerous VoCs and fatigue was accumulating among team members, and I started thinking about how to solve this problem and how to handle it more efficiently. Requesting support for VoC response Actual VoC response does not end with writing a single sentence in response. You should read the inquiry, check the policy document, look at the code base, search the DB if necessary, and if you still have difficulty deciding, ask the question again to a team member who knows the context well. In other words, VoC response is a task closer to inquiry and judgment than writing. Recently, there are customer service automation tools with various AI functions. However, what the team needed was not a general answer generator, but a way to access the policies, code, data, and context of our service and query and query like an actual team member. What was the problem? There were three problems. First, a small team had to deal with more than 10 incoming VoCs per day, both in time and context switching costs. Second, each team member had different contexts, so there were differences in the basis for confirmation and the method of answering. If I couldn't immediately remember where to find the evidence I needed, I had to ask another team member. Third, even if we tried to automate answers, it was difficult to access the internal context with just the general AI answer function. In real-world operations, this limitation was evident because grounded responses were more important than natural sentences. So there were two problems we wanted to solve. One was an issue of operational efficiency that enabled more VoCs to be handled with fewer people, and the other was an issue of quality that reflected the actual service context and internal rationale. To achieve this, the Moonlight team structured a processing flow to collect VoC, explore the required evidence, draft it, and finally have it reviewed by a human. Agentic VoC processing flow The first thing I focused on while working on this project was structuring the response process. The first thing I thought about was “what should I look at first?” When I summarized the steps that team members repeatedly take when responding, the overall flow was as follows. Check existing conversation history Check policy document Check the code base DB inquiry if necessary Draft your response The first goal was to make this flow reproducible for agents. The second goal was to ensure that responsibility still lies with humans. Because automatic sending was burdensome in terms of quality and security, we configured it to have agents do the searching and drafting, and have humans make the final decision. This is the commonly referred to human-in-the-loop method. So this project wasn't just an answer generator, it was more about creating a processing flow to collect VoC, explore evidence, create a draft, and send it out for human review. Moonlight VoC processing flow Customer Inquiry Customer inquiries come into the VoC channel. Customer inquiries are received through webhooks, and cycle inquiry is used as an auxiliary method to recover from omissions. Determine whether the VoC is eligible for processing. For processing purposes, the evidence is explored in a sequence similar to the human flow. - View embedded existing conversations - View code base and policy documents - Database query if necessary Create a draft response based on navigation results and templates. Forward drafts to Slack. Draft response displayed in Slack 7. After the person in charge checks the summary and details of Slack, select a processing method through the Send, Retry, and Deny buttons. When you click the Send button, it is sent to the VoC channel Results of applying agentic flow The biggest change after applying this structure is that team members no longer have to personally carry out the response process from start to finish. Previously, people had to read the inquiry, decide what evidence to check, find the necessary information, and organize the draft. On the other hand, for about 50% of all inquiries, agents first handle the repetitive inquiry and drafting stages, and the person in charge only has to make the final decision in Slack. This change wasn't just about response speed. We were able to reduce the differences in context for each team member and also lower the cost of “who should I ask this to?” In particular, by combining existing conversation history, policy documents, code base, and data inquiries into one flow, we were able to make the process of finding evidence more consistent. So, rather than a system that automatically sends responses, this project was positioned as an operational tool to reduce the costs of repeated exploration and drafting during the VoC response process. Connection with MoonClaw Basic VoC collection, evidence search, draft creation, review, and sending can be accomplished using the VoC processing flow described previously. However, in actual operation, there are cases where this flow does not end. Sometimes you need to tweak the draft a bit, and sometimes you need to double-check the project context related to the current inquiry. There are times when you want to check again to see if the basis the agent came up with is correct even by the current standards. In this case, what I am writing above is MoonClaw It is. MoonClaw is not a tool that replaces the VoC processing flow itself, but rather a tool that operates on top of it. Supervisor Agent Close to . Normally, the VoC processing flow handles most inquiries, and only talks to MoonClaw to refine the results when additional confirmation or correction is needed. For example, a team member might ask MoonClaw to “Please double-check only the payment policy portion of this answer against the latest code.” Or, you can review once more whether the basis referenced by the agent is still valid in the current operational context and whether certain expressions are consistent with actual policy. In other words, if the VoC processing flow is the basic path, MoonClaw can be seen as a path that accepts exception situations or additional queries above it. The biggest advantage of this structure is Operational Flexibility It is. Independent Engine: The VoC response flow handles routine tasks reliably 24 hours a day without the need for a higher-level agent. Interactive reinforcement: Only when complex judgments or modifications are necessary, we communicate with the agent through MoonClaw and refine the results. Share context: Since MoonClaw holds the context of the entire project, it can ensure response in accordance with the update direction of the entire service beyond simple VoC processing. Through loose connection with higher-level agents, routine processing is automated and complex problems are solved through collaboration between agents. Scalable operating structure has been equipped. finish The biggest thing I gained from this deployment was a sense of how the agentic system can share the work of humans. Rather than replacing all human work, we found that it was effective to divide it into exploration, gathering evidence, drafting, and approval, and then entrust the repeatable parts to the system first. On the contrary, it was clear that things had to be left to people until the end. It was still up to humans to decide whether to actually send it to the customer, determine what context to add in exceptional situations, and take responsibility for incorrect responses. So, this project was not about automation that eliminates people, but was closer to creating a structure that reduces repetitive tasks while maintaining responsibility for people. In the end, this project was a case of experimenting with how actual operational tasks could be transferred to an agentic system, rather than VoC automation itself. For the Moonlight team, this experience went beyond simple response automation and became a standard that could be applied when dealing with other operational areas in the future.

title: Corca AX Day
description: We introduce AX Day, where the Corca team, who is serious about AX (AI Transformation), immerses themselves in AX for 6 hours a day, experimenting and collaborating.
author: 김선영(HR Manager)
searchText: Corca AX Day 2026-03-18 Sunyoung Kim (HR Manager) Introducing AX Day from the Corca team, which is serious about AX (AI Transformation). The beginning of AX Day Corca made bold choices to drive 10x growth. right away Decided to devote 6 hours of a designated day entirely to AX It is. In the short term, this is a decision that will result in devoting approximately 20% of total work time to AX. But Corca used this time as more than just an experiment. Investment to create greater productivity and new possibilities in the long term I am looking at it. In order to actively respond to a rapidly changing environment by breaking away from existing methods, That's how AX Day started. grow together AX Day is not an individual task, Paired Collaboration Approach It proceeds as follows. We share bottlenecks we experience in each other's work processes, define problems from our own perspectives, and explore solutions together. This process naturally leads to not only individual growth but also improvement of the capabilities of the entire team. Additionally, AX is not necessarily limited to work. Some teams implemented a visualized music player, while others created a custom metronome that could be used while running. In this way, AX Day is a time that crosses the boundary between work and daily life and allows for various attempts. It starts with a small experiment, but the experience will lead to greater possibilities. Team members' stories about AX Day The team members’ reactions were enthusiastic🔥 Below are actual reviews from team members who participated in AX Day. There are agentic engineering sharing sessions, but it was great to be able to closely observe how other people treat AI and how they use it through AX Day pair work. And looking at the results of the first AX Day, I can see that each person has different thoughts, so I am looking forward to seeing what kind of results we can create on the next AX Day! It was fun to have the opportunity to collaborate with other teams and solve problems together. I think it was also a day where I was able to get away from my daily work and ventilate. It was nice to be able to get a glimpse of other people's ideas and things I'm interested in recently. Time passed by more quickly than I thought. In particular, it was my first time using OpenClaw, and I think I was able to approach AI a little more easily through an interesting topic. I thought that this kind of time could have a better impact on the future. I think AX Day was a time to become more familiar with AI. I want to develop it further and create an agent that can be used in practice! Even after lunch and AX Day ended, stories about AX continued throughout the team. We each shared what we had tried and talked naturally about what to do next. Although it was a short time, it was meaningful in that we were able to try new ways and see each other's approaches up close. I’m looking forward to seeing what other results will come out at the next AX Day🚀

title: Our new beginning, Corca moving story
description: We introduce how Corca redesigned the way we work and stay together by moving to an independent office.
author: 김선영(HR Manager)
searchText: Our new beginning, Corca moving story 2026-02-02 Sunyoung Kim (HR Manager) Corca has moved to a new space. In the meantime, I've gone through really small and frequent moves. Starting with shared office rooms for 6 people, rooms for 12 people and up to 30 people. As the number of team members increased by one or two, the space also grew little by little. This time for the first time independent office has been moved to . (Wow👏👏👏) This move is not just a matter of changing locations, It was also a process of thinking once again about how we work and what kind of time we want to spend. I'm going to tell the story! Why did I move? The previous office was Corca's first start, and it was a precious space where many moments were shared. However, I gradually began to feel the limitations in capturing the future direction and growth. So we decided to find a space that could accommodate both the current Corca and the future Corca! New office, so different Introducing Corca’s new office! Based on an overall bright and warm atmosphere, we wanted to create a comfortable space for long stays. office entrance The first thing you do when you come in is corca logo You can see it. The logo was placed in front of the entrance so that Corca can be seen naturally at the beginning and end of the day. The first space to be introduced is office space It is. We organized the place so that we could communicate naturally, while respecting each person's flow and concentration. I thought the most important thing was the balance of being able to talk right away when necessary and maintaining each person's rhythm when immersion was needed. I thought we needed a space where we could rest well and have good conversations, rather than a space where we could only work. Lounge and rest area Have a quick chat over a cup of coffee, This is a space where you can cool off by yourself for a while. The open kitchen is also designed to allow people to sit and chat freely, allowing communication outside of work to continue naturally. made of transparent glass meeting room was designed so that the space does not feel stuffy. When there is an external meeting or important discussion, it can be used privately using a curtain, making it a space that can be used flexibly depending on the situation. A word from the team members “I can concentrate much better than before, and the atmosphere has become more comfortable.” “I think the feeling of working has become new because the space has changed.” “I’m looking forward to what will happen here in the future.” In this space we How much more can we learn and how much can we grow in this new space? This office allows Corca to try more, fail more often, and I think it is a starting point that can help you become stronger. As I spend more time in this space, I look forward to Corca’s future even more 🚀

title: Live Activity, a deeper look: Real-time schedule function developer
description: We outline how the Trace team implemented a real-time scheduling experience using iOS Live Activity and ActivityKit Push and Push to Start.
author: 김성훈(iOS Product Engineer)
searchText: Live Activity, a deeper look: Real-time schedule function developer 2025-12-10 Seonghun Kim (iOS Product Engineer) Hello! My name is Seong-Hoon Kim, and I am working as an iOS Product Engineer on the Trace team. Most apps these days bombard users with push notifications to get their attention. But we all know. Notifications are easily ignored, buried in the scroll, and don't do their job when you need them. Conversely, Live Activity experiences that change delivery status in real time on the lock screen, such as Coupang Eats or Baedal Minjok, naturally capture the user's attention. You can immediately see where you are and how much is left without having to open the app, and this flow makes the user experience even more seamless. In fact, Apple also introduces Live Activity as a function optimized for providing ‘continuously changing information’ such as schedules, exercise, delivery, movement, and timers. There was a similar request from a user in the Trace Discord community. “I want to see at a glance what I need to do today without having to open the app.” “I wish I could see the current schedule on the lock screen.” So Trace began experimenting in earnest to see how this request could be addressed through Live Activity. In this article, I will try to organize step by step what problems Trace encountered in the process of implementing a real-time schedule experience using Live Activity and how they were solved. APNs On iOS, all pushes are delivered through Apple Push Notification service (APNs). The push types sent by APNs can be broadly divided into three types. General notification (Alert Push) Banner/sound/vibration notification visible to user Silent Push (background push) Used to briefly wake up the app and execute background tasks without appearing on the screen. ActivityKit Push (Push Live Activity Updates) A dedicated type used to directly update the state of a Live Activity. When I first started adding Live Activities, it was not clear how ActivityKit Push operates and what data structure it requires. So naturally, I started with Silent Push, which I have been using a lot and am familiar with for background updates. “Wouldn’t it be possible to quietly wake up the app and just update Live Activity?” This was the easiest approach I could think of. But after trying several times, I realized one important thing. Live Activity is not a structure that simply sends an “update request,” but must send the entire data (contentState) that needs to be displayed on the screen at that time through a push. In other words, the server had to directly calculate and send “what schedule is currently in progress and how much is left,” and it was difficult for the Silent Push infrastructure to reliably meet this demand. Automatic update of Live Activity The core idea was simple. “Without waking up the app, let the server calculate the status of Live Activity and send it as a push.” Because Trace uses Firebase instead of a separate server infrastructure, This structure could be implemented relatively simply with just Firebase Functions + Firestore. To update your Live Activity ‘as if in real time’, there are only two things you need: A place to calculate what schedule is currently in progress A pathway to push the results directly into Live Activity 📌 Entire data flow iOS App └─ (토큰 + 일정 저장) ↓ Firestore ↓ Firebase Functions └─ 현재 이벤트 계산 ↓ APNs – ActivityKit Push └─ contentState 전송 ↓ Live Activity └─ UI 자동 업데이트 1) iOS Obtain a liveActivityToken automatically issued when creating a Live Activity Save time-based calendar list to Firestore With this information alone, the server can know “on which devices Live Activity should be updated, and by what schedule.” 2) Firebase Functions Functions run every minute and do the following: View currently valid device list in Firestore Select ‘events to be shown now’ based on the current time among the events of each device Put the event in contentState and send ActivityKit Push to liveActivityToken // ActivityKit Push 전송 const notification = new apn.Notification(); notification.topic = "com.example.app.push-type.liveactivity"; notification.pushType = "liveactivity"; notification.event = "update"; notification.contentState = { event: currentEventData, // 지금 보여줄 이벤트 timestamp: Date.now(), status: "Active" }; await apnsProvider.send(notification, liveActivityToken); 3) Operation video This shows how the entire flow works in practice: the server passes contentState to ActivityKit Push, and the Live Activity reflects it immediately. Push to Start The Live Activity structure so far has had one premise. The thing about Live Activities is that they only start when the user directly enters the app and triggers it. But we wanted to go one step further. “Couldn’t Live Activity start automatically even without turning on the app?” The function that makes this possible is pushToStartToken, introduced in iOS 17.2. pushToStartToken allows you to remotely start a Live Activity on a server. This means that even if the user doesn't open the app: When an important event starts soon Just before the task begins Even when the user hasn't turned on the app at all during the day. You can create an experience that automatically displays Live Activity on the lock screen. For reference, Apple's documentation explains that you can receive a token from pushToStartTokenUpdates even without creating an Activity. However, on iOS 17.x this behavior was quite unstable. There have been reports that several developers have experienced the same problem, and Apple is also known to be aware of the issue. For this reason, Trace adopted a method of securing tokens by briefly creating a ‘dummy activity’ that is invisible to the user when running the app. (This behavior is said to have been improved in iOS 18.) 📌 Entire data flow iOS App └─ (pushToStartToken + 시작 시각 저장) ↓ Firestore ↓ Firebase Functions └─ 시작 시점 도달 여부 확인 ↓ APNs – Push to Start └─ attributes + contentState 전송 ↓ Live Activity └─ 앱을 열지 않아도 자동 생성 Push to Start Once you get the tokens, the overall structure is very simple. 1) iOS Through dummy activity when running the app pushToStartToken acquisition Save pushToStartToken and schedule start time to Firestore 2) Firebase Functions Functions run every minute and do the following: Check “Is now the time to start Live Activity?” When the point is reached, APNs pushToStartToken Send a Push to Start request to . At this time, the attributes and contentState that will be initially displayed in the Live Activity are also passed. // Push to Start - Activity 생성용 Push notification.event = "start"; // "update"가 아닌 "start" notification.attributes = { // 생성 시에만 필요 startTime: Date.now() }; notification.contentState = { title: "러닝 30분", eventDate: 1733458200, status: "active" }; In other words, like update push, the server calculates all the data to be displayed on the screen and sends it all at once. 3) Operation video You can check the process by which the server remotely creates a Live Activity using pushToStartToken and sends the attributes and contentState to be initially displayed so that it automatically appears on the lock screen. finish Since introducing Live Activity, Trace's key indicators have also changed. In particular, Day0 → Day1 retention increased by about 10%, showing a clear improvement in the initial experience. Users gave feedback saying, “It’s convenient to be able to check things right from the lock screen without having to open the app,” and “I like being able to complete my to-do tasks right away in Live Activity.” Trace will continue to deeply explore the capabilities of AI and the iOS ecosystem and create experiences that naturally permeate users' daily lives. The process of building this Live Activity was a very interesting challenge for the team, and it was an experience where I personally gained a lot of insight. I hope this article will be of some help or reference to those who are thinking about a similar problem. thank you reference material Starting and updating Live Activities with ActivityKit. push notifications—Apple Developer Update Live Activities with push notifications.—.WWWDC23 pushToStartToken—.Apple Developer https://developer.apple.com/forums/thread/805324

title: App Store Optimization: Marketing's New Growth Formula. Secure stable inflow to your app through app store optimization
description: We introduce how to reduce dependence on paid advertising through App Store Optimization (ASO) and create stable inflow by improving keywords and store pages.
author: 유현명(Product Engineer)
searchText: App Store Optimization: Marketing's New Growth Formula. Secure stable inflow to your app through app store optimization 2025-09-26 Hyunmyeong Yoo (Product Engineer) App Store Optimization: Marketing’s New Growth Formula Marketing costs exceeded this month again. CPI continues to rise, ROAS falls... This is a problem that any product owner or marketer has probably experienced at least once. Paid advertising costs rise every year, users become insensitive to advertising, and click-through rates gradually drop. The cost-effectiveness of paid advertising is decreasing every year. Moreover, the moment you turn off paid advertising, the inflow stops as if you turned off the faucet, making it a difficult marketing method to secure a stable inflow. But here's an interesting statistic. According to Apple 65% of keyword searches lead to downloads in the App Store , and Users acquired organically have an average 25% higher long-term retention than users acquired through paid advertising. is true. (Source: App Radar, ASO) The reason is simple. Because organic users felt the need and searched for, found, and installed the app, Users have much higher expectations and willingness to use the app than users who installed it impulsively after seeing an advertisement. . Just as a restaurant you visit in person stays in your memory longer than a place you learned about through an advertisement. 1. App Store Optimization: The Hidden Key to Product Growth App Store Optimization (ASO) is an optimization strategy that increases app visibility and downloads in the App Store and Google Play. Like your website's SEO, it includes all activities that increase your search rankings in app stores and improve conversion rates. Traffic × Conversion Rate = Number of Downloads traffic increase : Help more people discover your app (keyword optimization) Conversion rate improvement : Ensure that people who discover it actually download it (store page optimization) In order to download an app, 99.9% of users must go through the App Store. No matter how good an advertisement you create, users ultimately make their final decision by looking at the App Store page. In this article, for traffic, App Store Optimization (ASO) Let’s take a look. 2. ASO Practical Guide: Getting Started with Data Step 1: Find out your current location (ASO Audit) Before starting ASO, we must first understand the current state of our app. Key indicators to check: What keywords are you currently ranking for? Are actual downloads occurring for each keyword? What is our app’s visibility score compared to competing apps? How does your current conversion rate compare to the category average? 💡 ASO tools like AppTweak, App Annie, and Sensor Tower can help you understand this data at a glance. In particular, AppTweak’s ‘ASO Score’ shows the app’s optimization level on a scale of 0–100, allowing you to quickly find improvement points. Step 2: Keyword Research—The Treasure Hunt Begins 🔍 Effective keyword discovery process: Start with brainstorming Words that describe the app's core features Problems users want to solve Terms actually used by users (review analysis) 2. Competitor analysis Keywords used by top ranking competitive apps Niche keywords that your competitors are missing Utilize the ‘Keyword Spy’ feature of ASO tools 3. Prioritize keywords Keyword Score = (Search Volume × Relevance) ÷ Competition This is the process of finding the most appropriate keywords that will lead to app downloads. If the competition is less intense and the keywords match the needs of the app, the conversion rate will be advantageous! 3. Does our app compete with Coupang on the ‘shopping’ keyword?: How to survive among large platforms “How do we get involved in keywords that are taken over by all major corporate apps?” This is the most sighing question I hear when doing ASO consulting. Big keywords are already tightly controlled by large companies. But here's the twist. Big keywords (e.g. “shopping”) : Monthly search volume 1 million+, all top 10 MAU 1 million+ large company apps, probability of a new app being in the top 10: less than 0.1% Long-tail keywords (e.g. “men’s clothing shopping”) : Monthly search volume 5,000, conversion rate 3 times higher In fact, since it is a specific keyword, it has a high probability of leading to a download (conversion rate). Users who search for ‘shopping’ often just browse, but users who search for ‘men’s clothing shopping’ have a clear need to purchase men’s clothing right now. If you run a used trading app, it is realistic to start with long-tail keywords such as “used college textbooks” and “used baby products” instead of highly competitive keywords such as “used trading” and start ASO with a solid conversion rate and less competition. In order to discover long-tail keywords, it is important to discover expressions frequently used by users in our app and specific target/situation keywords that competitors ignore. 💡 real case : A meditation app started with long-tail keywords like “sleep meditation” (8,000 searches, 45 competition) and “5-minute meditation” (3,000 searches, 30 competition) rather than “meditation” (50,000 searches, 95 competition) and increased organic downloads by 250% in three months. 10 small but reliable markets go 1 large uncertain market It's better than 4. Timing is everything: Seasonal keyword strategy “The diet app I created will be downloaded in January. 10 times “It’s increased, but we didn’t do anything?” Sometimes, there are apps where you experience this kind of ‘good luck’. But it's not luck seasonality It is innate. One of ASO’s hidden weapons is Seasonal keyword strategy It is. Year-round ASO calendar New Year (3-day resolution season) : Golden time for habits, diet, exercise, and education apps • Keyword examples: “planner”, “schedule management”, “home training” March (new semester) : Demand for education, productivity, and shopping apps surges • Keyword examples: “timetable”, “study timer”, “student discount” June - August (summer vacation) : Preparing for summer vacation and travel • Keyword examples: “accommodation reservation”, “flight reservation”, “exercise” December (end of year) : Apps related to dating, shopping, and reflection • Keyword examples: “date”, “year-end shopping”, “new year goals” 💡 It is important to preoccupy the keywords of the target season in advance with search keywords that frequently occur by season, such as those mentioned above. ASO Since the effect usually takes more than two weeks, the key is to calculate the product's keywords in advance in preparation for the season. Slow Food Marketing, ASO fade marketing If it's fast food, ASO is slow food. There are no immediate results after implementation, but once established, it ensures a continuous and stable inflow. Even without burning advertising costs, many new users come voluntarily every day. “ASO is not a one-time project. The app store algorithm continues to change, and the competitive environment also changes. But because of that, there are even more opportunities.” Of course, it is good to eat all foods equally, so it is most important to carry out appropriate marketing according to the situation of the product. I hope that all of your services are successful, and thank you for reading this long post! reference material AppTweak. “What is app store optimization: Your guide to ASO in 2025” App Radar. “What is App Store Optimization (ASO)? The in-depth guide for 2024”

title: 2024 Corca Walk in Busan
description: Corca leaves for Busan! The Corca team went to Busan on a work trip from August 26th to August 30th! Workation is a compound word of work and vacation, and is a type of work whe...
author: 김선영(HR Manager)
searchText: 2024 Corca Walk in Busan 2024-09-13 Sunyoung Kim (HR Manager) Corca leaves for Busan! The Corca team went to Busan on a work trip from August 26th to August 30th! Workation is a compound word of work and vacation, and is a type of work where you work while taking a break wherever you want. This was my first time going on a work trip, so I was half excited and half worried, but everyone on the team was very satisfied with the results! I was able to go on a happy walk. Let's take a look at Corca's first walk, from the preparation process to the 5-day, 4-night walk together. Corca leaves for Busan! Are you going on a walk? As this was our first workshop, the expectations of the team members were very high. In order to meet this expectation, we formed and prepared a work TF team. Thanks to the TF team, we were able to have the ‘best work’ and ‘a fruitful work’ by making group tees, making placards, preparing breakfast, a place for company dinners, and things to do. I would like to take this opportunity to thank you once again. Workspace prepared by the TF team / Corca 2024 SS T-shirt Workspaces and accommodations with high satisfaction 😊 Introducing accommodations and workspaces with high satisfaction during this walkthrough The Holiday Desker Work Center Yeongdo, where you can work while looking at the sea! It was a very healing place just being able to see the sea in front of me while working 👍. The accommodation was comfortable for one person per room. The view of Yeongdodaegyo Bridge from the window was also the best. Workspace with a view of the sea / comfortable accommodation Alignment day Did you just go to Busan and work? no! Through the alignment day on the first day, I was able to build precious memories with my team members and become closer. Among them, the most impressive one for me personally was the ‘Let’s Praise Corca’ time. It was a meaningful time where we could praise each other and convey stories and gratitude that we couldn’t normally express! It was so touching... 🥹 In addition to this, we also played a Sudoku game where we divided roles and thought about how to generate profit, and we also played Corca Golden Bell, and a prize was given to the last 🎁 person. Let's praise Corca Enjoy Busan🌊 There are so many foods that come to mind when I think of Busan! Corcan people, who are serious about taste, explored delicious Busan food every lunch. I was able to have a happy time eating new and delicious lunches with my team members every day. You can’t come to Busan and not eat fresh seafood and meat, right? The restaurant tour with team members continued in the evening as well. As the number of Corca team members has increased, it has become more difficult to hold a company dinner in Seoul than before, but during this work trip, we had a great time having a company dinner for the first time in a long time and having a birthday party for a team member who had a birthday in August. 😋 There was a side effect of gaining weight after working out, but when traveling, you should eat without worrying about your weight. Busan, where my mouth was happy Vivid work reviews from team members The Corca team members had a great time in Busan. Let’s find out what this work period was like through the review. It was my first time trying a program called Workation, and I really enjoyed being with my teammates in a new space. It was refreshing and all the facilities were great. Thank you very much to everyone who worked hard for the work. I was glad that the pit stop, travel, and unity all went well in Busan! It was nice to talk about things I normally couldn’t. It gave me an opportunity to reflect on the way I work again. I had so much fun playing with my teammates. I made a lot of good memories. The accommodations were nice, the surrounding scenery was nice, there were a lot of good restaurants, and it was nice to play with my teammates, reorganize teamwork, and relax. Goodbye to Busan. There are many cases where we don't look back at each other when we only look ahead and run, but during the 4 nights and 5 days Busan walk, we were able to look back at each other and gain the strength to run more energetically. It was a walk where I was able to feel the meaning of ‘us’ once again! Please look forward to what event we will have next. Corca contributes to the development of human civilization by ensuring that everyone enjoys the benefits of technological advancement.

title: Want to Join Corca?. Introduction to Corca Team Page
description: Corca's team page We would like to introduce you to the new (corca.team) where you can learn more about Corca! Corca Recruitment Page Corca's team page can be visited through th...
author: 류가형 (Product Manager)
searchText: Want to Join Corca?. Introduction to Corca Team Page 2023-04-28 Ryu Ga-hyung (Product Manager) Corca's team page We would like to introduce you to the new (corca.team) where you can learn more about Corca! Corca Recruitment Page Corca's team page can be visited through the link corca.team! Corca, a team that changes the world we live in with AI technology, corca.team How is Corca's team page structured? Corca’s team page consists of 1. About Corca, 2. Corca’s Culture, and 3. We’re Hiring. About Corca Introduction to the Corca team You can check out About Corca. The introduction of the Corca team has been organized into a total of 3 menus to provide a more detailed introduction to the Corca team. You can check the origin of Corca's name, Corca's Clients/Partners, and Investors. (2) Corca’s Solution You can check the structure and operation flow chart of the Corca development solution and the introduction process, introduction effect, and success stories of introduction. (3) Corca attracts media attention. You can see an article about Corca attracting free investment worth 100 million won in March 2023 3 70 A. Corca’s Culture Corca’s culture Corca’s Culture contains an introduction to . (1) How Corca works You can get a glimpse of Corca's working environment, which supports members' learning and growth, supports an environment in which they can immerse themselves, and supports members who will work together. (2) Corca’s core values Corca's Mission, Vision, and Goal can be found in Corca's core values. (3) Corca team story There is an interview with People of Corca team members where you can hear about the atmosphere and project of the Corca team directly from Corca team members. It is updated regularly, so please visit and meet the Corca team members in person! We’re Hiring You can see the positions being recruited and Corca's contact information and location on We're Hiring. (1) Recruiting positions In the job list by team, by job, and in the positions being applied for, the Job Description, Job that Corca is currently hiring for. method You can check the recruitment process from start to finish interview. (2) Contact Us At Contact Us, you can check Corca's website, blog, contact information, and company location. Other than his team page, how can I get to know Corca? In addition to Corca's team page, there are two main ways to get to know Corca. Tech Tech Culture Culture This is the Corca team blog that you are currently reading. Articles on various topics such as , , etc. are regularly uploaded to the blog. Team members are free to write about topics they want to share. Coffee chat/call chat with Corca team members You can apply for Coffee Chat or Call Chat to a Corca team member before joining Corca. The application link can be found on the Corca team page. Visit Corca’s team page (corca.team) now to learn more about Corca! Corca is always open 🫶. If you have any further questions about Corca, please let us know and we will actively reflect them on the team page and blog! Corca contributes to the development of human civilization by ensuring that everyone enjoys the benefits of technological advancement.

title: ‘Buddy program’ to help you adapt to new corkine. Introducing Corca’s onboarding culture, the ‘Buddy Program’.
description: In this article, I would like to introduce the ‘Buddy Program’, a part of Corca’s onboarding culture! Corca has an Orcabot that helps with onboarding from the first day of emplo...
author: 류가형 (Product Manager)
searchText: ‘Buddy program’ to help you adapt to new corkine. Introducing Corca’s onboarding culture, the ‘Buddy Program’. 2023-02-17 Ryu Ga-hyung (Product Manager) In this article, I would like to introduce the ‘Buddy Program’, a part of Corca’s onboarding culture! Corca has an Orcabot that helps with onboarding from the first day of employment. (If you are curious about Orcabot, please refer to the link below!) Hello Corca, Hello Orcabot Introducing the Corca onboarding bot, Orcabot Orcabot onboarding is conducted to get to know all team members by talking directly with them and completing quests together. But it's another thing to have the friendliest team member with whom you can talk about daily life and regularly ask how you are doing after joining Corca, whether you are adjusting well and whether you have any concerns, isn't it? Corca’s Buddy Program That’s how ‘(Buddy)’ started. The Buddy Program, which is conducted to help new team members who have joined Corca comfortably adapt, is a time when matched team members meet regularly and share various stories. Corca's culture with It is. By learning about Corca's culture and through the Buddy Program, new members can build rapport with Corca team members who are colleagues and whom they can rely on personally as mentors. Buddy matching criteria There was concern that if team members from the same job category were matched as buddies, we would definitely talk a lot about projects and work 😂 So, rather than talking about work, we are matching buddies from different occupations so that we can get to know each other more comfortably by talking about overall company life and each other. For example, those who joined the company as a Product Manager will be matched with an ML Engineer, those who joined the company as an ML Engineer will be matched with an Operation Manager, those who joined the company as a Dev Engineer will be matched with a Product Manager, and those who joined the company as a Data Scientist will be matched with a Dev Engineer! Existing team members who are already participating in the buddy program can restart the buddy program for new team members once the ongoing buddy program process is completed. 😉 So you can focus on one buddy at a time Any member who has been with Corca for more than 3 months can participate in the buddy program as an existing team member, regardless of age/experience, and everyone is happy to participate! Systematic buddy program implementation Are you curious about what we talk about during our multiple meetings with Buddies? For those who may be having difficulty, we are providing simple guidelines that are not required but can help 🥰 Check-in topic Day 1: On the day of joining, introduce the buddy program and say hello. After 1 week: Lunch to check if you have adapted well during the week, if there were any difficulties, and if you have any questions. After 1 month: Coffee chat time to discuss how you felt about working at Corca for a month, whether you had any questions, whether there are tasks you want to do/learn at Corca, etc. After 3 months: Follow-up meeting after the one-month check-in - Talk about project details, share dreams/goals, etc. and ask for help. It's time to talk about what we can give, but as a result of my personal participation, after meeting several times, an internal intimacy has built up, so simple questions such as 'Have you adapted well to Corca?', 'Are you having any difficulties?' Anything you two people have in common can be a topic of conversation in the buddy program! Lunch and coffee chat meal support Lunch, lunch support, coffee chat, cafe expenses. If you join the buddy program, of course, we support +. All you have to do is post it on the Slack channel as shown in the picture below and have a private time! Interviews with team members who participated in the buddy program I heard firsthand the stories of team members who participated in the buddy program. It was a really fun time to hear vivid reviews and episodes about how helpful the buddy program was 😆 Stories from existing team members Did the buddy program help you get closer to your new team members? Corgi found out that Buddy was dealing with similar concerns that he had when he first joined the company, and it served as an opportunity for them to talk better and become closer. He said that he wished he had had a buddy when he joined the company as well. Were there any memorable stories or episodes from the buddy program? Squirrel shared an interesting episode! He said he was impressed by the way the newly hired birdie ‘seriously imagined what she would be doing if she were Mansour’s daughter.’ 😂 These buddies are rumored to have played balance games for over 30 minutes since their first meeting. New team members’ stories Did the buddy program help you adapt to Corca and become closer to your teammates? In addition to the Corca project, I was able to hear stories about daily life, which helped me a lot in adapting. Alpaca said yes. It was good that the psychological distance from other team members was reduced. He first became friends with Buddy and told us about it! Were there any memorable stories or episodes from the buddy program? I found things in common and found people I knew in the same way. Bee says she started doing it while having a coffee chat with her buddy! What would you like to see added to make the buddy program more active? Flamingo, who has a lot of experience in various workplaces, shared his experience at his previous company and gave great feedback! After joining the company, it is mandatory to take turns eating with different teams every day for two weeks. Buddies diligently work together with their own team + other teams for a month for Coffee Chat. Rather than having a meal or coffee alone between a new employee and a buddy, the buddy takes the lead in meeting with other team members. Thank you again to all team members who participated in the interview. ! We introduced the buddy program that is in progress for new team members who have joined Corca. Below are photos taken and sent directly by Corca team members while conducting the buddy program! 🥰 Can you feel the harmonious and warm atmosphere of Corca? In order to create a buddy program that both existing and new team members can enjoy and be satisfied with, we actively reflect the feedback and suggestions that team members share, and we are creating Corca's culture together with our team members today. Next time, we will come back with an interesting article introducing Corca’s culture! Corca, a team that changes the world we live in with AI technology, is creating new value based on advanced technology and planning ability. If you are interested in joining Corca's journey, please check out the Corca recruitment page! Corca contributes to the development of human civilization by ensuring that everyone enjoys the benefits of technological advancement.

title: Hello Corca, introducing ‘Orca-bot’
description: First day. The first day at a new school. The first day at a new company. Or the moment you meet someone for the first time. We face many ‘firsts’ in our lives, but the first ti...
author: J
searchText: Hello Corca, introducing ‘Orca-bot’ 2022-05-20 J First day. The first day at a new school. The first day at a new company. Or the moment you meet someone for the first time. We face many ‘firsts’ in our lives, but the first time is not always easy. On my first day at Corca, I went to work with a mixture of excitement and nervousness. Actually, I think I was more nervous than excited because it was my first time going to work in a long time. Isn't the reason why it's difficult to go to work for the first time because the space and people are new and awkward, and I don't know what to do first? Onboarding Program Therefore, each company awaits new employees through ‘onboarding’, which introduces them to the company and helps them adapt. In general, it is either boring and awkward training where new employees are gathered in a conference room and relayed throughout the day, or it is training where they have to sit alone and understand the content using PowerPoint. So what was your first day in Corca like? When I received the PC and set it up, “Orcabot” started talking to me. Who is Orcabot? (Orca-bot) ? Orcabot Corca's unique bot service (Orca-bot) helps make onboarding fun and effective. In particular, I was even more surprised to hear that this Orcabot was a service that Corca employees created one day while discussing an effective onboarding process. Among Corca's internal services, there are many interesting services created with such bright ideas. (I will introduce this step by step!) Then start Orcabot! After receiving the first message from Orcabot, the first person in charge came to greet me without even having time to think, ‘Who should I go to?’ The first quest is a must-have quest on your first day of work. Level up your basic knowledge of Office and now move on to the next quest. Quests are slightly different depending on the job, but the quests requested from me are broadly categorized as follows. Introduction and setting of the work system used at Corca Sharing Corca’s culture and values Introduction to duties and company basics What I liked about Corca onboarding through Orcabot is as follows. You can meet various Corca companion quests. You can quickly adapt to the work environment by directly helping with the settings needed for work. Step-by-step onboarding can be done step by step rather than one-time cramming. There are different people in charge for each core quest, so each person does their best to introduce their role. It is convenient because it can be carried out according to the situation of the employee and person in charge. Google Meet Gathertown and Corca's onboarding was even better because it could be carried out flexibly through , even in today's COVID-19 environment where people work repeatedly in the office and from home. (This is a new world that has never been experienced before!) So, shall we hear the story of another Corcain who onboarded with Orcabot? I asked Octopus, who joined Corca a few days before me, about Orcabot onboarding. Q. How did you feel about onboarding with Orcabot? A. I felt awkward and nervous when I first came to the company, but after onboarding with Orcabot, I was able to enjoy it without any pressure, as if I was completing a game quest! And as I talked to other people while doing quests, it was nice to be able to naturally learn the faces and names of my team members. Octopus also said that the onboarding program through Orcabot lowered the tension of his first day at work, and that onboarding was done in a fun way, so he was able to get it into his head and understand the content that Corca wanted to convey. The setup quest, especially Github, was said to be good because it allowed you to learn about Corca's well-organized development rules and how to actually use it by completing quests through simple tasks. The first reason why both Octopus and I liked Orcabot onboarding was that it was a combination of a bot and a person who could meet Corcaine in person, not just what the bot delivered! ‘Onboarding’, like ‘first impression’, is very important in creating an image for the company. “We are faster at developing technology than anyone else, but the people are bright, active, and warm. “” This is the first impression I got of Corca during onboarding. I have just completed onboarding at Corca and am starting to enjoy and look forward to my work life here. How about you? Did you want to come to Corca and meet the Orcabot I met? If you have any questions about Corca or would like to work with Corca, please visit the Corca recruitment website now! Orcabot and we are waiting for you. Corca contributes to the development of human civilization by ensuring that everyone enjoys the benefits of technological advancement.

title: Is everyone eating? “Is everyone eating?”
description: “Is everyone eating?” How often do you hear this? These are very kind and nice words, but strangely enough, I don't think it's easy to hear them often these days. In Corca, Orca...
author: 한충환 (MLOps Engineer)
searchText: Is everyone eating? “Is everyone eating?” 2022-05-20 Choong-Hwan Han (MLOps Engineer) “Is everyone eating?” How often do you hear this? These are very kind and nice words, but strangely enough, I don't think it's easy to hear them often these days. In Corca, Orcabot asks twice a day. Are you all eating? “ ?” At 10:30 a.m., Orcabot is speaking kindly to me again today. Corca has a #bob channel on Slack. In response to the friendly questions Orcabot asks on this channel, what if you decide whether to eat or not, or express your desired menu using various emoticons? We are organizing groups of members who will eat together today. If you are curious about the development history of Orcabot, please also read Part 1 of Orcabot development history. Whether at school or work, there is a saying called ‘food friend.’ I think anyone who has ever wondered, ‘Who should I eat with today?’ will know the importance of ‘food friend.’ At Corca, you don't have to worry about friends because Orcabot lets team members know who they will eat with so that they can eat with a variety of people. What people want to eat every day may not be as good as MBTI, but it does seem to tell a lot about a person's tastes or mood that day. In that sense, having the same taste is a very convenient and good thing. 🥗 There are members who always eat salad for lunch 🍣 If you especially want to eat sushi or spicy octopus today, These are Corcans who are looking for members they want to work with on the #bob channel. 👀 Is there a reason why Corcas are so serious about food? This comes to mind, there is a saying that people who know a lot want to eat a lot. “ ” . I think the Corca people are very curious and passionate people, so they are sincere about their food! I think another reason is Corca’s generous food support and “ ”. One of Corca's benefits is that generous lunch and dinner meals are provided during working hours. In particular, at Corca, voluntary tech seminars are held in the evenings and some members also study, so there are quite a few members who work in the evenings. Therefore, there are many cases where we eat dinner together and work together instead of the typical company working hours of 9 to 6, and since both lunch and dinner are covered, it is a work environment where team members can eat more comfortably and work hard. I've seen large companies run in-house cafeterias to provide meals to their employees, or provide a certain amount of meal expenses in their salaries, but I was very surprised that they provide employees with a generous limit for one meal every time! I think that’s why Corcans enjoy ‘meal time’ with a joyful and happy heart and burn with passion during work hours. I think that mealtime has an important meaning, going beyond just eating something to satisfy hunger, it also allows you to build human bonds by sharing your concerns about work and life with the people you eat with. A friend of rice is a real friend 😆 . We are waiting for many people to become ‘Jjinchin’ who eat with Corcans. Corca, a team that changes the world we live in with AI technology, is creating new value based on advanced technology and planning ability.
